text:
Baka Ki El Dogra (バカ＝キ＝エル・ドグラ, Baka Ki Eru Dogura), also known as Prince Baka (バカ王子, Baka-ōji—lit. "Stupid Prince"), is the main character of Yoshihiro Togashi's Level E.

Appearance

Prince Baka has wavy white hair and green eyes. He wears a black fur-trimmed coat. Although—canon to his own series—he has blond hair and blue eyes.

Plot

Heavens Arena arc

Prince Baka makes his cameo appearance in the 2011 anime adaptation. During the match between Gon and Hisoka, the announcer Cocco questions the referee's decision on giving Gon a critical hit, which gave Gon the lead 2-1 on Hisoka. Cocco tells the audience that Hisoka didn't feel the impact of Gon's punch, and then, out of nowhere, Prince Baka mysteriously appears beside Cocco and explains that referees are giving points based on what pleases them. He explains that some referees award points based on the damage inflicted, while others award points based on techniques and strategy. He also explains why Gon received a critical point decision, based on how he used a flagstone to hit Hisoka. When Cocco was about to ask who he was, Prince Baka mysteriously disappeared.

Togashi has not confirmed that he is Prince Baka. It may be an interpretation of himself, since he has used long hair, as in the Nippon Animation version.

Manga Appearances

Trivia

In the anime's credits, he was listed simply as the Mysterious Commentator (謎の解説者, Nazo no kaisetsusha).

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 1999 anime adaptation, instead of Prince Baka making a cameo appearance, a character known as Togashi which is also the name of the author of Hunter × Hunter appeared during Gon and Hisoka's match in the Heavens Arena. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 42 (1999) 
In Chapter 62, a guy with long hair also appeared beside the announcer, though it's unknown if he was Prince Baka or Togashi.

Miscellaneous

Prince Baka shares his Level E voice actor with the 2011 anime adaptation voice actor of Hisoka.
